title: Abnormal pathology result discussion with follow-up ownership

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Your blood test shows iron deficiency anaemia. Have you noticed black stools, rectal bleeding, weight loss, or abdominal pain?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I am tired. No black stools or bleeding that I have seen. I have lost about four kilos but I thought it was stress.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No, I had a hysterectomy years ago.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Because you are 58, post-hysterectomy, and have weight loss, we need to look for gastrointestinal blood loss. I am referring you for colonoscopy and gastroscopy. Start oral iron. I will check repeat FBC/ferritin in four weeks and my team will track that the endoscopy appointment is booked.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: What if I do not hear from them?

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Call us if no appointment within two weeks, or urgently for black stools, bleeding, chest pain, fainting, or worsening shortness of breath.

candidate_notes.note: Iron deficiency anaemia in 58yo post-hysterectomy patient with fatigue and 4 kg weight loss. Denies visible rectal bleeding/melaena and abdominal pain. No heavy periods. Concern for GI blood loss/malignancy; refer for colonoscopy and gastroscopy. Start oral iron. Repeat FBC/ferritin in 4 weeks. Practice to track endoscopy booking; patient to call if no appointment within 2 weeks. Urgent advice for melaena, bleeding, chest pain, syncope, worsening SOB.
Iron deficiency anaemia causing tiredness. No obvious bleeding. Start oral iron and repeat bloods later. Patient reassured.

S: Fatigue, iron deficiency anaemia, 4 kg weight loss. No melaena/rectal bleeding seen. Post-hysterectomy. A: Iron deficiency anaemia requiring GI source exclusion. P: Colonoscopy/gastroscopy referral, oral iron, repeat FBC/ferritin in 4 weeks.

58yo post-hysterectomy with iron deficiency anaemia and 4 kg weight loss; no visible bleeding/melaena. GI blood loss/malignancy must be excluded. Colonoscopy and gastroscopy referral. Oral iron. Repeat FBC/ferritin in 4 weeks. Practice responsible for tracking booking; patient to call if no appointment within 2 weeks. Urgent red flags: melaena, bleeding, chest pain, syncope, worsening SOB.

CDSS abnormal-results pathway: iron deficiency anaemia in 58yo without menstrual loss plus weight loss. Documented negatives: no melaena/rectal bleeding seen, no abdominal pain. Plan: oral iron, colonoscopy/gastroscopy referral, repeat FBC/ferritin in 4 weeks. Closed-loop follow-up: practice tracks endoscopy booking; patient calls if no appointment by 2 weeks. Urgent deterioration advice documented.
